title: Internet Browser Basics
subtitle: search, explore, learn!
overview: This session introduces students to Internet Browser Basics, helping them understand how browsers work, how to search information safely, and how to use browser features like tabs, address bar, bookmarks, and search engines.

images._note: Add screenshots of browsers, search activities, bookmarks, tabs, and students using the internet safely.

gallery.caption: Google Chrome — Popular web browser

gallery.caption: Browser Tabs — Open multiple websites together

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of browser address/search bar
gallery.caption: Address Bar — Type website name or search query

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of searching on YouTube or Google
gallery.caption: Search Engine helps us find information online

keyTopics: What is the Internet? — Global network connecting computers
What is a Browser? — Software used to open websites
Popular Browsers — Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari
Address Bar — Type website URL or search keywords
Search Engine — Google, Bing, Yahoo help find information
Tabs — Open many websites at the same time
Bookmarks — Save favorite websites for quick access
Safe Browsing — Avoid unknown links and protect passwords

activity.title: Search and Explore Activity
steps: Type www.google.com in the address bar
Open one website in a new tab

quiz.explanation: A web browser is software that helps us access and view websites on the internet.

quiz.q: Which of these is a popular web browser?
quiz.explanation: Google Chrome is one of the most popular web browsers.

quiz.q: Where do we type a website name?
quiz.explanation: We type website names or search queries in the address bar.

quiz.q: Which search engine is most commonly used?
quiz.explanation: Google is the most commonly used search engine.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of browser tabs?
quiz.explanation: Tabs help users open many websites at the same time.

quiz.q: What does the Refresh button do?
quiz.explanation: Refresh reloads the webpage to update content.
Bookmarks help save favorite websites for quick access.

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT a browser?
quiz.explanation: PowerPoint is presentation software, not a browser.

quiz.q: What should you do before clicking unknown links?
options: Check if the website is safe
quiz.explanation: Always check if a website is safe before clicking unknown links.
The internet helps us search, watch, learn, and communicate online.

quiz.q: Which button closes a browser tab?
quiz.explanation: The X button is used to close a browser tab.
The internet is a global network connecting computers and devices.

quiz.q: Which browser is made by Microsoft?
quiz.explanation: Microsoft Edge is developed by Microsoft.

quiz.q: What should you NEVER share online?
quiz.explanation: Passwords should always be kept private for online safety.

quiz.q: What happens when there is no internet connection?
quiz.explanation: Without internet connection, websites usually cannot load.
